above the spared area
2. Transient form
Continuous disease starting from the rectum, may also show rectal sparing
Biopsies sometimes focal; signs of chronicity or architectural distortion may be absent.
Usually in young children with short symptom duration.
3. Cecal involvement
Left-sided disease starting from the rectum with inflammation at the cecum and a healthy segment between the two areas
Typical; biopsies from the involved area may show non-specific inflammation
4. Upper GI tract involvement
Erosions or small ulcers in the stomach, neither serpiginous nor linear
Diffuse or focal gastritis, without granulomas (except pericryptal)
5. Severe acute colitis
Continuous disease starting from the rectum
May show transmural inflammation or deep ulcers; other typical features. Absence of lymphoid aggregates, V-shaped fissure ulcers.
